name: John Doe
history: History of substance abuse and completed rehabilitation. Ongoing family difficulties involving the relationship with his mother, described as rejection and emotional neglect. Longstanding obesity since childhood. Past experiences of bullying related to weight.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: If I don't meet expectations, I will be rejected.
intermediate_belief_depression: I can't change my situation, so there's no point in trying.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of situations that might lead to rejection or failure.
situation: Missed an alarm and woke up late for work.
auto_thought: I'm going to get in trouble and everyone will see me as unreliable.
behavior: Rushed to work in a state of panic, possibly leading to mistakes.

name: Case Study: Family Dynamics and Approval Seeking
history: Recent family tension related to a conversation with parents about a wedding list. The patient noticed similarities between their own actions and their father's patterns. The father is described as repeatedly seeking approval and unconditional love from family members.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: I must prove my worth to be accepted.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I don't meet expectations, I will be rejected.
coping_strategies: Seeking approval and validation from others, being defensive or aggressive when challenged.
situation: Observing the father's ongoing pursuit of approval from his sister.
auto_thought: I need to prove myself to be accepted.
behavior: Becoming defensive or aggressive in conversations, seeking validation.

name: PhD Candidate
history: PhD candidate engaged in scientific research. References to a sequencing-related study and a colleague named Kelly. Working on a project with a senior scientist who has struggled to gain recognition in his career.
intermediate_belief: I must work harder to be recognized.
intermediate_belief_depression: I am not good enough compared to others.
coping_strategies: Overworking to compensate for feelings of inadequacy.
situation: Comparing themselves to colleagues who work longer hours and publish more papers.
auto_thought: I will never be as successful as my colleagues.
behavior: Working excessively long hours and feeling anxious about productivity.

name: Patient X
history: Recent shoulder surgery with a painful recovery process. Ongoing pain, numbness, and tingling related to a herniated cervical disc. Prior history of physical therapy for past walking difficulties and back issues. Past prolonged illness associated with a period of immobility. Started a new relationship and later experienced a return of physical symptoms.
intermediate_belief: I must endure pain and limitations without complaint.
intermediate_belief_depression: My physical limitations define my worth.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of activities that might exacerbate pain, reliance on others for support.
situation: Attending a Halloween party while in persistent pain.
auto_thought: I won't be able to enjoy the party because of my pain.
behavior: Withdrawn and less engaged in social activities.

name: John Doe
history: Worked overseas and built a website related to that work. Recurrent pattern of starting new ideas and later abandoning projects when difficulties arise. Family-related difficulty involving his father; wrote a personal letter as part of a forgiveness process.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: I must always be in control to avoid failure.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't maintain control, everything will fall apart.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of difficult tasks and situations to prevent perceived failure.
situation: Reflecting on past achievements alongside current challenges.
auto_thought: I can't handle this; I'm going to fail.
behavior: Avoids engaging deeply with new projects or relationships.

name: 28-year-old student
history: 28-year-old student. Returned from Egypt. Previously relied on parents for support and is now managing bills, finances, and health insurance independently for the first time. Brother remains financially dependent on parents.
intermediate_belief: I must handle everything on my own or I am a failure.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't manage my finances and health insurance, I am a burden.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of dealing with financial and insurance issues due to feeling overwhelmed.
situation: Received an insurance claim with an unexpected balance.

behavior: Avoids making necessary phone calls and taking steps to resolve insurance issues.

name: John Doe
history: History of long work hours in demanding work environments, including past touring work. Unresolved issues with father, including reports of past abuse. Recent exchange of letters with father addressing this history.

intermediate_belief: I must work hard to prove my worth.

name: Patient X

intermediate_belief: If I confront Ashby, it will lead to a conflict that I cannot handle.
intermediate_belief_depression: I am responsible for maintaining peace, even if it means sacrificing my own comfort.

I am bound to be rejected

history: PhD student in a science field. Deep involvement in music; previously led a college band and currently participates in a jazz group. intermediate_belief: I must avoid taking on too much responsibility to prevent failure.

auto_thought: I can't handle the pressure of a leadership role right now.
behavior: Declined the leadership position in the jazz group.

name: Patient X

intermediate_belief: I must always be there for others or I will be alone.

name: John Doe

coping_strategies: Avoidance and withdrawal when feeling overwhelmed or criticized.
situation: An incident at a social gathering resulted in an emotional outburst among friends.
auto_thought: I don't understand why people are upset with me.
behavior: Withdrawal from social interactions and attempts to apologize without resolution.

name: Case Example
history: Forms close relationships with friends who experience significant stressors. A friend is dealing with academic pressures and parenting an autistic child.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: I must support my friends to maintain relationships.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't help my friends, I am a failure.
coping_strategies: Avoids confrontation and withdraws from relationships to prevent conflict.
situation: Multiple friends are distressed at the same time, and the patient is involved in supporting them.
auto_thought: If I don't help, they will leave me.
behavior: Withdraws from friends when feeling overwhelmed.

name: Case Study Example
history: Master's degree in engineering and experience in robotics. Past remorse related to not dedicating enough time to a project.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: If I try to pursue relationships or projects, I will fail and be rejected.
intermediate_belief_depression: I am not capable of succeeding in relationships or work, so why bother trying?
coping_strategies: Avoids taking risks in personal and professional life to prevent failure and rejection.
situation: Sent an email to someone they are interested in dating and received no response.
auto_thought: This lack of response confirms that I am not worthy of attention or success.
behavior: Withdraws from social interactions and avoids pursuing new opportunities.

name: Patient X
history: In a relationship with Ivan for about seven years and married for three. Stressful periods during academic finals and wedding preparation due to lack of support from Ivan. Current financial pressure involving Ivan's part-time work, therapy costs, and dental treatment expenses.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: I must be self-reliant because I cannot depend on others.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I don't manage everything perfectly, everything will fall apart.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of confrontation and procrastination in addressing issues.
situation: Ivan's mother speaks to the patient in a critical manner.
auto_thought: I can't handle this criticism; it means I'm failing.
behavior: Avoids interacting with Ivan's mother and delays addressing financial issues.

name: Patient X
history: History of complex and strained family relationships. Father described as frequently creating misery for those around him and expressing dissatisfaction with his life. Maternal grandmother had a significant negative impact on the family, especially on the patient's mother.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: I must control my emotions to avoid feeling vulnerable.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I show my emotions, I will be seen as weak and will be rejected.
coping_strategies: Suppress emotions and maintain control to avoid vulnerability.
situation: Reflecting on the potential death of the father and the grandmother.
auto_thought: I must not show my emotions or I will lose control.
behavior: Suppresses crying and emotional expression.

name: Case Study: Ethan
history: Longstanding friendship with Ethan since high school (about 14 years). Shared personal crises and professional collaborations. Learned that Ethan has been abusive toward his girlfriend.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: If I confront Ethan, I will lose his friendship.
intermediate_belief_depression: I can't change the situation without losing something important.
coping_strategies: Avoid direct confrontation and hope the situation resolves itself.
situation: Struggling to contact Ethan directly.
auto_thought: If I reach out to Ethan, it might ruin our friendship.
behavior: Avoidance of direct communication with Ethan.
